panel.llmNotReady: Plugin not ready: no valid API key
notebooks.title: Notebooks
app.error.llmNotConfigured: LLM plugin is not configured. Set the API key in the OpenRouter widget.
app.deleted.title: Deleted branches
app.fork.title: Fork ({count} branches)
app.fork.hint: ←/→ or mouse · Ctrl+←/→ — to the edge · Enter / ↓ / click — enter branch · Ctrl+↑/↓ — further along the tree · F2 — rename
app.root.label: Conversation start
app.composer.placeholder: Type a message... (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for a new line)
app.composer.attach: Attach a file to the request
app.artifact.attachHint: Insert a file into the conversation
widgets.compressor.title: Compressor
widgets.compressor.inactive.settingsOpen: Settings are open in the center
widgets.compressor.settings.title: Compressor settings
widgets.compressor.settings.intro.1: The compressor shrinks a selected part of the conversation using a separate LLM — not the one running the main dialog.
widgets.compressor.settings.intro.2: Place markers on model replies, choose start and end in the right panel, then click Compress.
widgets.compressor.settings.intro.3: Before attaching, you will see a preview in the center and can approve or cancel.
widgets.compressor.settings.intro.4: Below, choose the provider and model for compression and edit the instruction (prompt) if needed.
widgets.compressor.preview.title: Compression preview
widgets.compressor.error.apiKeyMissing: API key for {backend} is not set
widgets.compressor.error.providerNoKey: API key is not set — configure it in the provider plugin
widgets.compressor.previewPending: Preview in the center — confirm or cancel.
widgets.compressor.needMarkers: Need at least 2 markers on the branch.
widgets.compressor.prompt.default:
Write a short summary of the conversation. Cover the important points, facts, and conclusions. Remove intermediate reasoning, preambles, fillers, and the like. Do not quote the user's questions verbatim, but use them as the backbone of the narrative. Output length should be 25–30% of the source.

If there are intermediate code blocks that were revised or extended later in the text, you may remove them.
Do not modify or shorten complete code blocks. Copy them into the answer in full.
Preserve verbatim: code blocks, numbers, versions, identifiers.
Return ONLY the summary text, with no preamble or explanations.
widgets.compressor.prompt.userPrefix: Below is a conversation fragment to compress ({count} chars).
widgets.tagger.title: Tagger
widgets.tagger.inactive.settingsOpen: Settings are open in the center
widgets.tagger.settings.title: Tagger settings
widgets.tagger.settings.intro.1: The tagger analyzes a selected part of the conversation using a separate LLM — not the one running the main dialog — and suggests tags.
widgets.tagger.settings.intro.2: Place markers on model replies, choose start and end in the right panel, then click Extract tags.
widgets.tagger.settings.intro.3: Before saving, you will see the suggested tags in the center and can approve or cancel.
widgets.tagger.settings.intro.4: Below, choose the provider and model for the tagger and edit the instruction (prompt) if needed.
widgets.tagger.preview.title: Suggested tags
widgets.tagger.error.apiKeyMissing: API key for {backend} is not set
widgets.tagger.error.providerNoKey: API key is not set — configure it in the provider plugin
widgets.tagger.previewPending: Suggestion in the center — confirm or cancel.
widgets.tagger.needMarkers: Need at least 2 markers on the branch.
widgets.tagger.prompt.default: Analyze the conversation and describe it with 5–6 short topical tags. If a suitable tag exists in the dictionary, use it exactly (same spelling). If none fits, craft a new short tag. Tags must be in the language of the conversation. Return ONLY a comma-separated list of tags, with no explanations and no '#' symbol.
widgets.tagger.prompt.userPrefix: Below is a conversation fragment to analyze ({count} chars).
widgets.saveDialog.title: Save dialog
widgets.llm.modality.outputLabel: Output — what the model must return
widgets.llm.modality.inputHint: Soft filter: the model must accept the checked items (it may support more).
widgets.llm.modality.outputHint: Hard filter: the model must produce everything that is checked.
widgets.llm.favorites.noKeyFirst: First set an API key on the “API Key” tab.
widgets.llm.favorites.empty: No favorite models. Mark the ones you need on the “All models” tab.
widgets.llm.favorites.modelHint: Choose the model to process the dialog. “−” removes it from favorites.
widgets.llm.maxTokens.label: Max response length (tokens)
widgets.llm.maxTokens.hint: Cap on the model's response length (max_tokens). Default {default}. Higher means longer answers and more quota usage.
widgets.llm.allModels.hint: Check models to add to favorites — they will appear on the first tab.
widgets.llm.apiKey.replaceHint: Enter a new key to replace the current one.
widgets.llm.apiKey.saveHint: Paste the key and click “Save key”.
widgets.openrouter.title: OpenRouter
widgets.openrouter.settings.title: OpenRouter settings
widgets.openrouter.settings.keyInstructi: Get an API key at openrouter.ai/keys and paste it below. The key is stored in the system keychain.
widgets.openrouter.panel.noKey: Set an API key (⚙ top left)
widgets.openrouter.panel.modelsNotLoaded: Models not loaded. Check the key and connection.
widgets.openrouter.panel.notForDialog: Not for dialog (no text output). Choose another on the “Favorites” tab.
widgets.openrouter.panel.modelNotFound: Model not found in the list — choose another in ⚙
widgets.openrouter.panel.ready: Ready — select the radio in the header
widgets.openrouter.keyVerify.failed: The key failed verification — models were not loaded.
widgets.openrouter.error.noConnection: No connection to the OpenRouter server — check your internet.
widgets.openrouter.api.insufficientFunds: Insufficient funds for this model. Top up your OpenRouter balance or choose a cheaper model.
widgets.openrouter.api.parseFailed: OpenRouter: failed to parse the response. Body: {body}
widgets.gemini.title: Gemini (Google)
widgets.gemini.settings.title: Gemini settings
widgets.gemini.settings.keyInstructions: A free key from Google AI Studio. Sign in with a Google account, create a key, and paste it below.
widgets.gemini.panel.noKey: Set a free API key (⚙ top left)
widgets.gemini.panel.modelsNotLoaded: Models not loaded. Check the key and connection.
widgets.gemini.panel.notForDialog: Not for dialog (no text output). Choose another on the “Favorites” tab.
widgets.gemini.panel.modelNotFound: Model not found in the list — choose another in ⚙
widgets.gemini.panel.ready: Ready — select the radio in the header
widgets.gemini.keyVerify.failed: The key failed verification — models were not loaded.
widgets.gemini.error.noConnection: No connection to the Gemini server — check your internet.
widgets.gemini.api.rateLimit: Gemini free-tier limit exceeded (requests per minute/day). Wait a bit and try again, or choose another model.
widgets.gemini.api.keyInvalid: The Gemini API key is invalid. Check the key in settings.
widgets.gemini.api.forbidden: Access denied (403). Make sure the key is active and the API is enabled.
widgets.gemini.api.overloaded: The Gemini model is currently overloaded on Google's side (free tier). This is temporary — try again in a minute or pick a lighter model (flash/flash-lite).
widgets.gemini.api.safetyBlocked: Gemini blocked the response due to safety rules. Rephrase your request.
widgets.contextMeter.title: Context
widgets.tree.title: Tree
widgets.tree.empty: This conversation has no messages yet.
widgets.tree.inactive.noDialog: Open a conversation to see its tree.
widgets.tree.deletedNav: This branch is deleted. Restore it first to open it.
